The choice between Quicksort and Mergesort depends upon specific constraints regarding memory overhead and average-case time complexity. Quicksort generally exhibits an average time complexity of O(n log n) and benefits from superior cache locality; however, its worst-case performance is O(n^2). Conversely, Mergesort provides a stable O(n log n) performance regardless of input distribution but requires O(n) auxiliary space. Please define your memory limitations.
